Off Calendar Oct-14-2016 Continued To Jan-04-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Sep-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT HARVEST ON GEARY, INC.'S Motion For Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Defendant Harvest on Geary, Inc. is awarded $14,027.85 as reasonable attorney's fees and costs. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-19-2016 Continued To Nov-02-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Amended Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 425.16
Off Calendar Jun-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Harvest on Geary, Inc.'s anti-SLAPP motion to strike is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden on the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis. "Since the cause of action 'arose directly out of statements made and plans submitted to the planning commission and city council,' the defendants had satisfied the first prong of the anti-SLAPP statute." (M.F. Farming Co. v. Couch Distributing Co., Inc. (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 180, 195.) Here plaintiff Bernal Heights Cooperative, Inc. complains that defendant's permit application was not properly processed and should be revoked Plaintiff's sole claim thus arises from defendant's communications with the City permitting authorities which implicates protected activity of CCP 425.16. Plaintiff fails to satisfy its second prong burden of showing that its claim has minimal merit. The only evidence submitted by plaintiff to oppose the motion is a cursory declaration from Mr. DeVries that does not address plaintiff's prima facie case. Even considering the papers submitted by plaintiff on the injunction motion, those documents do not show the requisite minimal merit of plaintiff's claim necessary to defeat this motion. Plaintiff does not show how the City and/or the defendant acted illegally or how the City abused its discretion in allowing defendant to operate. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
